title: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: PRA: Autopsy and coroner’s reports
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 6250 et seq.) ("CPRA”), and all other applicable laws, please disclose the following records.

Any and all autopsy, death investigation, and coroner’s reports relating to the deaths of the following individuals:

- Name: Garrett Schmidt, Date of birth: 05-27-1983, Date of death: 10-27-2016
-  Name: Victor Johnson, Date of birth: 11-03-1963, Date of death: 08-02-2018
- Name: Stephen Murray, Date of birth: 01-12-1967, Date of death: 07-17-2019

If records pursuant to this request are possessed in machine-readable format such as an .xls, .csv spreadsheet file, please provide the record in this format. Please disclose these records in the format in which they are kept, e.g., .ppt, .pptx, .doc, or .docx.

The fundamental rule of the CPRA is a presumption of public access. “In other words, [A]ll public records are subject to disclosure unless the Legislature has expressly provided to the contrary.” Williams v. Superior Court, 5 Cal. 4th 337 (1993). This presumption finds further support in the California Constitution, as amended by Proposition 59 in 2004. “A statute, court rule, or other authority, including those in effect on the effective date of this subdivision, shall be broadly construed if it furthers the people's right of access, and narrowly construed if it limits the right of access.” Cal. Const. Art. 1 § 3(b)(2).

Please provide a determination on this request within 10 days of your receipt of it. If you determine that any or all of the information qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, please note whether, as is normally the case, the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. If you determine that some but not all of the information is exempt from disclosure and that you intend to withhold it, please redact it for the time being and make the rest available as requested. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any or all of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

Please apply a fee waiver to this request. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please provide notification of any duplication costs exceeding $50 before you duplicate the records. If the request for a fee waiver is denied, please further provide an index of all other requests since 2018 for which a fee waiver was denied, and the reason therefore.

Please respond to this request and disclose the records electronically by email to this MuckRock email address. Please let us know if you have any questions or if we can help ensure this request is the least burdensome possible for your agency while providing a correct and full disclosure in the public interest.

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out to me via email at dillon@muckrock.com, or by phone at (267) 314-7952.

Thank you.

Best,
Dillon Bergin

title: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, NMSA 1978, §§ 14-2-1 through 14-2-12, I request the following records from Luna County, including the Luna County Detention Center, for the period August 1–31, 2025.

The records described below are administrative records of booking and transfer logistics. They are not "law enforcement records" within the meaning of NMSA 1978, § 14-2-1.2(D), which defines that term as evidence received or compiled in connection with a criminal investigation or prosecution. ICE immigration detainers and transfer records are instruments of civil administrative enforcement, not evidence compiled in connection with a criminal investigation, and are therefore not subject to the exemption in § 14-2-1(D).

1. All ICE detainer forms (Form I-247, immigration detainer) received by the Luna County Detention Center during August 2025, including the date each detainer was received and the date of any compliance or refusal.

2. Release records, however maintained, for all individuals transferred to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody during August 2025, including: date of transfer and the release reason or code recorded in the facility's records management system (e.g., "ICE hold," "federal transfer," "immigration detainer"). If no single release log exists, please provide all records from which this information can be derived, including transfer paperwork and booking system entries reflecting ICE releases.

3. Any records, however maintained, reflecting ICE personnel entering the Luna County Detention Center during August 2025, including sign-in logs, visitor logs, or facility access records.

I am a reporter with Capital News Service and the Howard Center for Investigative Journalism at the University of Maryland, requesting these records in connection with ongoing reporting on ICE enforcement activity in New Mexico. This request is in the public interest.

I request a fee waiver on the grounds that these records contribute significantly to public understanding of how federal immigration enforcement operates through local detention facilities. If fees cannot be waived, please notify me before incurring any costs.

Please respond within fifteen days as required by § 14-2-8(D). If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon, provide a written explanation as required by § 14-2-9, and disclose all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of any withheld record.

Sincerely,

Raphael Romero Ruiz
Reporter | Capital News Service / Howard Center for Investigative Journalism
Philip Merrill College of Journalism, University of Maryland
7765 Alumni Drive, College Park, MD 20742
C: 520-971-7654
rromeror@umd.edu

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Raphael Romero Ruiz

communications.subject: Re: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: IPRA — Luna County ICE transfer and detainer records, August 2025
communications.communication:
Good morning-

It is the policy of the Sixth Judicial District Court (“6JDC”) to be fully 
responsive to all requests for public records available under the 
Inspection of Public Records Act, NMSA 1978, § 14-2-1 et seq. (“IPRA”). We 
have received your request dated May 5, 2026, requesting that the court 
provide the following, "Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public 
Records Act, NMSA 1978, §§ 14-2-1 through 14-2-12, I request the following 
records from Luna County, including the Luna County Detention Center, for 
the period August 1–31, 2025. The records described below are 
administrative records of booking and transfer logistics. They are not "law 
enforcement records" within the meaning of NMSA 1978, § 14-2-1.2(D), which 
defines that term as evidence received or compiled in connection with a 
criminal investigation or prosecution. ICE immigration detainers and 
transfer records are instruments of civil administrative enforcement, not 
evidence compiled in connection with a criminal investigation, and are 
therefore not subject to the exemption in § 14-2-1(D). 1. All ICE detainer 
forms (Form I-247, immigration detainer) received by the Luna County 
Detention Center during August 2025, including the date each detainer was 
received and the date of any compliance or refusal. 2. Release records, 
however maintained, for all individuals transferred to U.S. Immigration and 
Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody during August 2025, including: date of 
transfer and the release reason or code recorded in the facility's records 
management system (e.g., "ICE hold," "federal transfer," "immigration 
detainer"). If no single release log exists, please provide all records 
from which this information can be derived, including transfer paperwork 
and booking system entries reflecting ICE releases. 3. Any records, however 
maintained, reflecting ICE personnel entering the Luna County Detention 
Center during August 2025, including sign-in logs, visitor logs, or 
facility access records..”

We have searched our files and made inquiries within the court and have 
determined that there are no documents that are responsive to your IPRA 
request. 

6JDC is not the records custodian of the records you are requesting. 
Instead, we believe that the custodian of those records that you are 
requesting might be:

Luna County
IPRA@lunacountynm.us
700 S. Silver Ave., Deming, NM 88030
575-546-0494

Therefore, pursuant to NMSA 1978, § 14-2-8(E), we will be forwarding your 
request to the record custodian for further response. In addition, you may 
want to contact the record custodian to directly convey your public records 
request to that court.

We now consider your request closed.

Thank you,

Angela Rodriguez

title: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request

communications.subject: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Freedom of Information Act. I would like to request both that a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every facility managed or owned by Hawaii Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation. Please provide this information in a spreadsheet, if possible: 
* Overall staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Overall staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff budgeted positions (per prison, per month)
* Custody staff filled positions (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff overtime (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of overall staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Average hours of custody staff sick leave (per prison, per month)
* Overall population count (per prison, per month)
* Total number of infractions (per prison, per month)
* Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) (per prison, per month)
* Total number in restricted housing (per prison, per month)
* Total number of appeals from incarcerated individuals (per prison, per month)

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility managed or owned by Hawaii Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation. In order to help to determine my status to assess fees: I am affiliated with an educational institution (CSU), and this request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use. Thank you for your consideration of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessie Harney

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
Our office received the request below and will need additional time to gather the information. The information you seek is not readily available and will require a manual search, review, and segregation time.

Pursuant to HRS §92F, and the Office of information Practices Administrative Rules, §2-71- 19, when a person requests access to a government record under Part II of Chapter 92F, HRS, the agency may charge the requester:

(a) (1) Fees for searching, reviewing, and segregating the record; provided that these fees shall be assessed in accordance with this chapter; and
(b) An agency may require a requester to prepay the following before an agency begins the search for and review of records in order to respond to a request for access:

(1) Fifty percent of the total estimated fees for searching, for reviewing, and segregating records when the estimated fees exceed $30;
(2) One hundred percent of the estimated fees from previous requests, including abandoned requests, in accordance with subsection (d).

Accordingly, in any request for information to DCR, the following charges apply for responsive documents: 1) @2.50 for each 15-minute period of search time; 2) $5.00 for each 15-minute period of review time and segregation; and 3) $0.50 per page for documents copied/digitized.

For your request, we have prepared a billing estimate. If you wish to proceed, fifty percent of the billing must be received prior to gathering all information, and the remaining balance must be paid before the records are released to you. Attached are the payment instructions. Please review and let us know if you would like to move forward.


Mahalo,

Ashlynne Galzote

communications.subject: RE: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
Hello! 

Thank you for your reply! I appreciate it!

It appears that the budgeted positions are broken down by facility but not by month. For sick leave and overtime, these appear to be broken down by an employee code and month, but not by facility. 

Can this be provided at the requested level of facility and month for each measure? For example, in January 2024, Halawa had (mock numbers here), but 410 total staff, 10 hours of sick leave and 9 hours of overtime, on average.

Thank you!

communications.subject: Re: [EXTERNAL] RE: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: Hawaii DOC Prison-Month Level Data Request
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I received your inquiry. The data actually already contains what you are looking for, although it's admittedly not intuitive.

With regard to the budgeted position excel sheet, it has been broken down by month. The issue with the budget is that it is set on a fiscal year basis and does not change from month to month. For example, Fiscal Year 2024 applies for all of the months of July 2023-June 2024, as shown on the excel sheet. As a budget, those numbers remain true for all of the months in that fiscal year.

Similarly, the sick leave and overtime hours are already broken down by facility, and is identified using the budgetary codes as recorded by our government records. For example, PSD402ED refers to the level IV program PSD 402 Halawa Correctional Facility. PSD 403 refers to Kulani Correctional Facility, etc. Reference is made to the columns in the PDF - and through those codes you can identify all of the facilities and other programs where there was OT and SL. Admittedly this isn't explained in the PDF itself because we provide you with the government records as they are recorded in our system, so the supporting executive budget program structure explanation publicly available at https://budget.hawaii.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/09.-Public-Safety-FB-25-27-Executive-Budget-Program-Structure.7Lt.pdf  will help you to identify the facility or program codes.

Best,
-SL

title: Long-term unemployment statistics in Nevada

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Long-term unemployment statistics in Nevada
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Long-term unemployment (unemployed for 27 weeks or more) data in the state of Nevada from November 2025 to March 2026 or the most recent data. If you have percentages for each month, that would be preferred. If not, a total number of unemployed workers per month and a total number of long-term unemployed workers per month works fine.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Lynn Lazaro

communications.subject: RE: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Long-term unemployment statistics in Nevada
communications.communication:
Please see the update response below:

I’ve been going through to see what data we have.  Most is not what he wants.

To be honest, he’d be better off compiling his own statistics from the public data from the Census Bureau (but it’s not analysis we have a copy of to provide).  It’s all free and open, and can give him what he actually wants (to the extent that data is available to meet the request).
-DETR Amin ADMINISTRATION

communications.subject: Response to Records Request
communications.communication:
Please find attached the documents we found to be potentially responsive to your request for information.  I would also like to recommend the following sources for your consideration:


  1.  American Community Survey public use microdata: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/microdata/documentation.html  - This will allow you to do custom compilations of long-term unemployment on a year-over-year basis, including additional detail about the duration of unemployment and number of weeks worked in the last year.  This is the most robust survey on the topic I am familiar with.



  1.  Current Population Survey public use microdata: https://www.census.gov/data/datasets/time-series/demo/cps/cps-basic.html - This is the data for the monthly unemployment rate survey.  However, this data is likely not detailed enough in Nevada to have very good estimates at a granular level because the survey is just not large enough.  Nevada has about 800 households in the survey sample, of which (roughly!) 600 respond to the survey (based on response rate), 400 are in the labor force (based on the share of the population in the labor force), and 10 are unemployed (based on the unemployment rate.  Subsetting that very small number by long-term unemployment is tricky.


Please note:

  1.  The federal shutdown in October and November 2025 affected many of these series, since we publish the data on a rolling 12-month average basis.
  2.  The data presented in the Excel table here is the requested month-by-month data.  However, we would never publish this data in this form as it is too volatile on a month-to-month basis to be reliable. In it, the numeric estimates are thousands of individuals, but this data is not adjusted for seasonality or controlled for noise in the underlying survey.

David Schmidt
Chief Economist
Nevada Department of Employment, Training, and Rehabilitation
Email: deschmidt@detr.nv.gov
Phone: 775-684-0387
https://www.nevadaworkforce.com

title: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of the minutes for the Planning Board meeting held on March 2, 2026. This request is also made pursuant to the Massachusetts Open Meeting Law, M.G.L. c. 30A, §§ 18–25.

Please provide the minutes for this meeting.

If no minutes exist, please confirm that fact in writing.

This request includes any records that document the discussions, deliberations, and actions taken at the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

For context, I note that the Planning Board subsequently met on March 9, March 23, and April 6, 2026. During those meetings, it was stated on the record that no minutes were available. Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes are required to be created and approved in a timely manner, generally understood as within three meetings or 30 days, whichever is later. Based on these dates, the deadline for timely approval of the March 2, 2026 Planning Board meeting minutes would have been no later than the April 6, 2026 Planning Board meeting.

Under the Open Meeting Law, minutes of an open session are public records and must be made available upon request within 10 calendar days. If any portion of the requested records is withheld, please cite the specific legal basis for the denial.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eileen Kelleher

communications.subject: PRR 2026-40 RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request for Planning Board Meeting Minutes – March 2, 2026
communications.communication:
Good morning,

This office is in receipt of your request for public records information. We will be in contact with you if any changes are incurred when filling your request. Please be advised we have 10 business days to respond to your request or notify you that more time will be needed and any applicable fees.

Please feel free to contact us if you require any further assistance and have a great day!

Thank you, Amy

title: Data Dictionary

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Data Dictionary
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide a copy of the Data Dictionary for the BOP Deaths in Custody database at of February 2025.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

communications.subject: FOIA Request 2025-01983
communications.communication:
Dear Andrew Free, 
This is to acknowledge receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request regarding Data Dictionary for BOP deaths in custody. The Federal Bureau of Prisons (BOP) assigned your request FOIA Request Number 2025-01983. The records you seek require a field office to search for and collect records and/or we expect any records responsive to your request will be voluminous and require significant time to review, and/or your request requires consultation with at least one other agency with a substantial interest in your request. Because of these unusual circumstances, we are extending the time limit to respond to your request beyond the ten additional days provided by the statute. See 5 U.S.C. § 552 (a)(6)(B)(i)-(iii) (2018). For your information, we use multiple tracks to process requests, but within those tracks we work in an agile manner, and the time needed to complete our work on your request will necessarily depend on a variety of factors, including the complexity of our records search, the volume and complexity of any material located, and the order of receipt of your request.  At this time, we have assigned your request to the complex track. We anticipate processing your request will take up to 9 months.  In an effort to speed up our process, you may wish to narrow the scope of your request to limit the number of potentially responsive records so it can be placed in a different processing track. You can also agree to an alternative time frame for processing, should records be located, or you may wish to await the completion of our records search to discuss either of these options. Any decision with regard to the application of fees will be made only after we determine whether fees will be implicated for this request. We regret the necessity of this delay, but we assure you your request will be processed as soon as possible. If you have any questions or wish to discuss reformulation or an alternative time frame for the processing of your request, you may contact this Office by e-mail at bop-ogc-efoia-s@bop.gov by telephone at (202) 616-7750, or by writing to the above address. You may also contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Ms. Kara Christenson, at the same email or telephone number to discuss any aspect of your request. You can also check the status of your request online at http://www.bop.gov/foia/index.jsp#tabs-6. 
Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer.  The contact information for OGIS is as follows:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, Room 2510, 8601 Adelphi Road, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448. 
Sincerely, 
FOIA Staff 
Federal Bureau of Prisons 
U.S. Department of Justice

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2025-01983
communications.communication: I appeal. None of what you've said about these records (needing to search a field office / voluminous records) is true.

Respectfully,
S. Arellano
Government Information Specialist
Federal Bureau of Prisons
Office of the General Counsel

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #2025-01983
communications.communication:
Good afternoon, 

I appeal the decision in this case. 

I did not ask for the data. I asked for the Data Dictionary. You provided an incomplete dataset, but not the data dictionary. A more complete dataset is here: https://www.data-liberation-project.org/requests/bop-federal-inmate-deaths/. But not the data dictionary I asked for. 

Here is an example of a BOP data dictionary for another database: https://www.bop.gov/resources/research_projects/assaults/assault_documentation/datadictionary_assaults.pdf
And another example: 
https://oig.justice.gov/sites/default/files/2022-08/Vaccine-Dashboard-Data-Dictionary.pdf

Please provide the records I asked for? 

Thank you, 

Andrew Free

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Pierce County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.communication:
Hi Adrian,

Thank you for your quick response to my request!

In my request, I also asked for "accompanying impeachment information", which may be the material linked in the "LINK TO DISCOVERY" column. Would you please provide that as well?

Thank you,
Rose

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request #PCPAO Reference No. 26-0010
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

I had interpreted your request as only requesting current Brady or PIE lists. Thank you for that clarification. I will reopen your request to search for the “accompanying impeachment information” for the individuals on the lists that were produced to you on January 12.

At this time, we anticipate that the next installment of records will be available to you on or before Thursday, February 19, 2026. If we determine that additional time is needed to complete your request, you will be notified. Should you have questions or concerns regarding this request, please contact our office referencing PCPAO Reference No. 26-0010.

Thank you,
Adrian McDaniel
Public Records Officer
Prosecuting Attorney | Civil Division

RE: PIERCE COUNTY Prosecuting Attorney PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST, Reference # P041339-010526. 
 
Dear Rose Terse, 
A production of records responsive to your request is ready for release. These records meet the fee threshold, as outlined in Pierce County Code 2.04.030(E). Please see the attached invoice, INV26-P041339-2. 
 
To make a payment online, log back into your Pierce County Public Records Request portal account and select "View My Invoices". Select "Make a Payment" for this invoice and follow the prompts to enter your payment information. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXXndrKlkWAcXKs3RKk0x6-2B8pdCk0ipTOgEFrezx8dJEGTEGSWYfnvF8UlMzIRC67G-2BfU3pkPbhhu86G19FienkUzJeTLf3Wh6ipnaLWflqnq2Q2vSDVkavEiXYzyby6S43YxpWOfFDYeLU-2BC8CZGwVWRuvZ-2FJbCEjlDiyWLk-2F9VvHCgNWPTwfTwYy9oubLU52g-3D-3DjQw6_cZC2DE3u2cs2QgRIye4LE-2FUuF21C7Nw2WTsI40J0tkEB7tIy1FyNVop12O496qbghtbzawlLBdX57vx3EgqRGDjoqk-2BCuTKjCptvxqCnHHbl25SlzzhVusYnUayysjbEDX2b20VFJZEIFdnD8CgW1uIJOO6HpCHdpAIpoEp0T6WVqZZwA3OcooD-2BHBQN6DC7vPI5vAGNBGa1O25kkMxxvfalOJn6EdUDoAdvF1RCumSi1r7VjeIVYaAt6GpSZqTqg-2BJcktUQskyhylt9n1EJYcxJAy77Rc-2Bi5ek0unwcVuRT5w9gnWnHYaT5EXhgVbc4MPnDvEBY3g4cPXpzDJ-2BSyFxa-2FGE7M-2BYx0R0bx4HpT-2FPjKmcnXeSIyThvYgxpkI9-2B) 
Alternatively, you may remit your payment to the Pierce County address on your invoice. Please include a copy of the invoice or note the Reference Number of the request with your payment so that we may apply your payment to the appropriate request. If no reference number is noted, we may not be able to determine which request to apply the payment. For cash payments, exact change is required. Personal checks will not be accepted as a method of payment. Cashier checks or money orders must be made payable to Pierce County. Processing of payment may take up to one business day, regardless of method of payment. 
Responsive records will be released to you upon completion of processing of payment. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(4) and Pierce County Code 2.04.040(J) if you do not pay the deposit or any installment payment due for the requested copies within 30 calendar days after receiving this notice, by the due date on the invoice, this agency may stop providing further installments of records and close the request. 
 
Respectfully, 
Prosecuting Attorney

To submit a new Public Records Request to Pierce County, please visit the Public Records Portal and click the "Submit a New Request" button. RE: PIERCE COUNTY Prosecuting Attorney PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST, Reference # P041339-010526. 
 
Dear Rose Terse, 
A production of records responsive to your request is ready for release. These records meet the fee threshold, as outlined in Pierce County Code 2.04.030(E). Please see the attached invoice, INV26-P041339-3. 
 
To make a payment online, log back into your Pierce County Public Records Request portal account and select "View My Invoices". Select "Make a Payment" for this invoice and follow the prompts to enter your payment information. (https://u8387778.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.VEsWrlMiJDg0mXafq1mEXXndrKlkWAcXKs3RKk0x6-2B8pdCk0ipTOgEFrezx8dJEGTEGSWYfnvF8UlMzIRC67G-2BfU3pkPbhhu86G19FienkUzJeTLf3Wh6ipnaLWflqnq2Q2vSDVkavEiXYzyby6S43YxpWOfFDYeLU-2BC8CZGwVWRuvZ-2FJbCEjlDiyWLk-2F9VvHCgNWPTwfTwYy9oubLU52g-3D-3Dx-mP_cZC2DE3u2cs2QgRIye4LE-2FUuF21C7Nw2WTsI40J0tkEB7tIy1FyNVop12O496qbghtbzawlLBdX57vx3EgqRGMtYl-2F5XNkv9JqEofI3iIO3CNgDGhxJ3-2BEn9zevdxrzGpJW9NBm84XEgz6p0JpyBeOzCuWdpd6oyKpZdwf8-2FmoU-2FjHGwCm08tebtVz0wkb2doooR25943W0gulZDQ2XG-2BcyZsusGpdM-2FhnYbxw-2B72qyGHIRk3xwi41vU9YCfNfp7kN8stgDcp1NM33b1-2BXSdwiBajSlqXMfU6ydvACTj-2Fdl8VsJ5BEjZBAspYO7xLizJWWLOt5plVCENh5a9-2Bgl9v70AuAfB-2Fh6GgY-2FufeEXU00yGQqezVapACn6xGgicUCaPO4UM-2FFrxmynttVmLDpBcQ-3D-3D) 
Alternatively, you may remit your payment to the Pierce County address on your invoice. Please include a copy of the invoice or note the Reference Number of the request with your payment so that we may apply your payment to the appropriate request. If no reference number is noted, we may not be able to determine which request to apply the payment. For cash payments, exact change is required. Personal checks will not be accepted as a method of payment. Cashier checks or money orders must be made payable to Pierce County. Processing of payment may take up to one business day, regardless of method of payment. 
Responsive records will be released to you upon completion of processing of payment. Pursuant to RCW 42.56.120(4) and Pierce County Code 2.04.040(J) if you do not pay the deposit or any installment payment due for the requested copies within 30 calendar days after receiving this notice, by the due date on the invoice, this agency may stop providing further installments of records and close the request. 
 
Respectfully, 
Prosecuting Attorney

title: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to Taylor’s Lawn Service for the following contracts: #92296, #93874, #94082, #99573, #99575, #99576, #99577, #99578, and #105471.

Specifically, I am requesting any records reflecting performance issues, including cure or default notices, withheld payments, invoice deductions, disputed charges, and any written correspondence regarding deficiencies or service quality.

For purposes of identifying responsive records, this request includes communications containing the following terms: complain OR "corrective action" OR deficient OR missed OR "not complete" OR redo OR "re-do" OR rework OR unsatisfactory OR problem OR issue OR bad AND ("Taylor’s Lawn Service" OR "Taylors Lawn Service" OR "@taylors").

Please provide records in electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Drek Hawkins.

communications.communication:
Mr. Vreeland,

We will retract this request.

Thank you for your time.

communications.subject: Re: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Taylor’s Lawn Service Contractor Performance Records
communications.communication:
Mr. Hoover and Mr. Hawkins -

This email combines and concludes three separate Muckrock requests for records related to mowing contractors, received by me on March 31 and April 2, 2026.

The first request received on March 31 was for all documents pertaining to KMF III Services. A Muckrock representative and I discussed via email the labor-reimbursement fee, and Muckrock agreed to a fee on April 2, which I estimated would not exceed $300. City staff started gathering responsive records. Then an email from Muckrock on April 23 seems to have rescinded the request, but by that point, the City had already gathered documents and completed most of the labor. Therefore, I am including those records and prorating the fee accordingly to reflect staff time in gathering the records prior to the request being rescinded.

Muckrock on April 2 withdrew its March 31 request for records pertaining to Avalawn, so there was no labor and no fee related to this request.

Muckrock on April 2 requested Taylor's Lawn Service documents and agreed to a fee if less than $250.

Attached are these contract records:

#92296: C24-0021 awarded to KMF III for Phil Keith Training Center;
#99578: C-25-0063; Zone D Mowing & Maintenance; awarded to Carmichaels Lawn Care.
#105471: C-26-0095 (just awarded this spring); Grounds Maintenance for Scattered Landscaping and Smart Fix 40 Sites; awarded to KMF III.
#93874: C-24-0060 Baker Creek Maintenance; awarded to Taylor's Lawn Service
#94082: C-24-0058 Landscaping at Suttree Landing Park; awarded to Taylor's Lawn Service - service pulled back in-house after 1st year of contract.

The following 3 were all awarded to Taylor's Lawn Service on contract C-25-0062:
#99573: I40/James White Parkway Mowing & Maintenance;
#99575: Zone B Mowing & Maintenance;
#99577: Zone C Mowing & Maintenance


In addition, I have attached all email communications as requested, i.e., "any written correspondence regarding deficiencies or service quality."

I believe these are all records available that are responsive to your requests.

Your fee wound up being less than I'd estimated. The total fee is $116.77.

The City follows the labor-reimbursement schedule outlined by the Tennessee Office of Open Records Counsel in handling records requests. The fee for the first hour of labor is waived, but then your fee is the reimbursement for labor for each subsequent hour. In this case, after waiving the fee for the first hour of labor, this fee reflects 2 hours of Purchasing staff time (at an hourly salary of $32.06) and 1 hour of my time (hourly salary, $52.65).

If paying by check: Please mail a check, payable to the City of Knoxville, to this address:



City of Knoxville

c/o Communications Department

Eric Vreeland, Deputy Director

City County Building, 400 Main Street, Room 654C

P.O. Box 1631

Knoxville TN 37901





If you prefer to pay electronically, pls use this link:



https://pay.payitgov.com/knoxville-treasurydepartment<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/linkprotect.cudasvc.com/url?a=https*3a*2f*2fpay.payitgov.com*2fknoxville-treasurydepartment&c=E,1,ACe678Ao3-canoxwnBGq2q4nztV5_tFThRyDc1D21tBFf9gW9JDC9vGNGeMGmQS4CvDScVVZtuud1cMhMntjH5iviX7ELMQkvAKeE1Zvim4LiUwPtQK8BKK6LsdY&typo=1__;JSUlJQ!!NC3vCrM4DMxsVXds!B8qarbgQOzjWcyqfDLU8DCWbYtIrJRsSUgxB75aMetOkrJ0Xl9iykin7P5OXYXCi8qj_chWiykSCtAEUJfX48Yjh-criZyER67sJ$>



On the last line, where it asks for an invoice number, just type in “public records request.”



Please email me if an electronic payment is made, because I keep an Excel sheet and I circle back to any records requesters after 3 months or so if I’m not sure the fee has been paid.


Thank you for your patience as we gathered these records.

Eric Vreeland
Deputy Communications Director

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@ic.fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI's FOIPA Program is searching the FBI's indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 134 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 523 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,335 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 1,875 days for processing.

Respectfully,

title: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI Memos Trump Epstein (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the FBI memos referenced in this New York Times story: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/25/us/politics/trump-epstein-files.html Specifically, I seek a copy of any and all "F.B.I. memos summarizing interviews the bureau did in connection to claims made in 2019 by a woman who came forward after Mr. Epstein’s arrest to say she had been sexually assaulted by both Mr. Trump and Mr. Epstein decades earlier, when she was a minor." The FBI wrote four such memos, according to the New York Times report, but three of them have not been released publicly. We seek to obtain those three memos. For more information about this case, see the attached document. 

I am a reporter for The Nation magazine and other news outlets, and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied, I am willing to pay $25 for these records.

I seek expedited processing for this request. The information I seek could shed light on a matter of urgent and compelling public interest -- namely, the potential involvement of federal government personnel in the alleged sex trafficking operation organized by the late Jeffrey Epstein. This information is at the center of public discourse at this moment, and the Epstein case is a matter that involves the White House, Congress and numerous judicial bodies. It is imperative that the public obtain information on this matter in a timely matter, particularly given the corrosive effects the Epstein case has had on trust in government. For the reasons above, I ask that my request be given expedited processing because there is an urgency to inform the public about actual or alleged federal government activity, and I am a person primarily engaged in disseminating information. I intend to use the requested records to inform the public about actual government activity. I certify the foregoing to be true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

title: Edward T. Blake (July 31, 1945 — Aug. 6, 2025)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Edward T. Blake (July 31, 1945 — Aug. 6, 2025)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into forensic scientist Edward T. Blake (July 31, 1945 — Aug. 6, 2025). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2025/10/01/science/edward-t-blake-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an associate editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: Right to Know Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Right to Know Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e
See attached.  There is no data available for any of the requests prior to August of 2023 that I could locate.

title: Oath of Office (Office Of The Director Of State Courts, Wisconsin Court System)

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled (Office Of The Director Of State Courts, Wisconsin Court System)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The oath of office filed pursuant to Wis. Const. Art. IV, section 28 and Wis. Stat. section 757.02 for each of the following Dane County Circuit Court judges:
   a. Judge Stephanie R. Hilton (Branch 8)
   b. Judge John D. Hyland (Branch 14)
   c. Judge Stephen E. Ehlke
   d. Judge Frank D. Remington (retired — oath as filed during tenure)
   e. Judge Jacob B. Frost (Branch 9)
   f. Judge Rhonda L. Lanford (Branch 16)

2. The oath of office, appointment order, and any designation or authorization order for Court Commissioner Jason Hanson, filed pursuant to Wis. Stat. section 757.68, including:
   a. The original appointment order specifying the scope of authority
   b. Any oath of office filed
   c. Any bond filed
   d. Any order or designation specifically authorizing Commissioner Hanson to conduct contempt proceedings under Wis. Stat. Chapter 785
   e. Any standing order or local rule delegating contempt authority to court commissioners in Dane County

3. Any Dane County Local Court Rule or administrative order governing delegation of authority from circuit court judges to court commissioners for contempt proceedings.

4. The date each oath listed above was filed with the county clerk.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

title: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Alhambra Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Use of force data collection (2017-2025) cities 50-100k (Alhambra Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident location, area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Iowa Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Iowa Open Records Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Iowa Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Iowa Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: Responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Find responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education. Proposals were due on 6/7/2023.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Toni Thompson

communications.subject: PRR 25-034 Florida Sunshine Law Request: Responses to RFI 2023-46, Safe Schools LMS, issued by the Florida Department of Education
communications.communication:
Please accept this as the Department’s acknowledgment of your request for information.

Your request has been forwarded to Department staff for review and response. The Department will respond in a reasonable time based on the effort required to locate, review, and redact the requested information. Please be advised, requests are addressed in the order they are received.

The assigned number for this request is PRR 25-034.

I have attached the Department’s Public Records Request Guide. Thank you and have a great day.

Sincerely,

Public Records Unit
Florida Department of Education
325 West Gaines Street, Suite 1544
Tallahassee, FL 32399-0400
Phone: 850-245-9759
Email: PRR@fldoe.org<mailto:PRR@fldoe.org>

files.title: Florida Department of Education Guide to Public Records Requests _2025

communications.subject: Re: (PRR 25-034) Re: Florida Sunshine Law Request #PRR 25-034
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Thank you for contacting the Department of Education. We have completed the review of this request and all responsive documents will be provided to you by way of a ShareFile in a separate email (the documents are too large to send via email). These materials constitute all of the documents held by the Department of Education that relate to your public records request.

You should receive the email with access to the ShareFile in one to two hours. Please note, the file will be deleted in 7 days.

title: Ohio Public Records Request – Body-Worn Camera - Arrest of Paige Cox (Aug 23, 2022)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Ohio Public Records Request – Body-Worn Camera - Arrest of Paige Cox (Aug 23, 2022)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to the arrest of Paige Cox (born 06/08/1994) on August 23, 2022, by the Cincinnati Police Department.

The arrest followed a single-vehicle crash. Ms. Cox refused a chemical test after being read Ohio's ALS form, and was cited under R.C. 4511.19. The arrest occurred in Hamilton County, Ohio.

The involved officers were identified by the following badge numbers:

• Badge P895 – Arresting Officer
• Badge P492 – Witness Officer

The enforcement location was listed as 3201 Warsaw Ave, Cincinnati, OH 45205.

I am requesting:

All body-worn camera (BWC) and dash camera footage from officers P895 and P492 and any related patrol vehicles that were at the scene.

Please provide the records in digital format (via email or downloadable link). If any records are withheld, I request a written explanation citing specific legal exemptions under Ohio law.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

title: CI Staff Record Study

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: CI Staff Record Study
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the 29 November 1976 memorandum to Chief Information Management Staff, "CI Staff Record Study" from David H. Blee (Chief, Counterintelligence Staft) as well as records set a, b, d, e, f, g, h, and i described on pages two and three of the memo.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The 2022 annual budget report for the Office of Public Affairs of the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1674627-000
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to appeal the determination of FOIA request 1674627-000, in which I requested the budget of the FBI's Office of Public Affairs for 2022.

In your response, you sent me a link to a speech delivered in 2021 by the former FBI director before the Senate Appropriations Committee. While this speech does contain certain information about the FBI budget, it does not mention the Office of Public Affairs specifically and is therefore not responsive to my request.

I therefore believe that my request has not been fulfilled in accordance with FOIA. I reiterate my request for the 2022 annual budget report for the FBI's Office of Public Affairs.

Respectfully,

Brett Carollo

title: Sogo Shosha Report

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Sogo Shosha Report
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I'm requesting a Mandatory Declassification Review (MDR) under EO 13526 (Section 3.5) of the following report;
Japanese Trading Companies: Evolving Role in World Trade.

This report has previously been released (https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP84S00928R000100060003-4.pdf).

title: Anonymous + general hacktivists 2009-2018 (Defense Intelligence Agency)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Anonymous + general hacktivists 2009-2018 (Defense Intelligence Agency)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documents mentioning or relating to Anonymous (the hacker movement/collective, see below) or hacktivism (defined as hacking as a form of protest and/or activism) generated between 1 January 2009 and 1 January 2019, including but not limited to:

* Internal reports, bulletins, warnings and alerts relating to Anonymous or hacktivism
* Reports from the appropriate IT (Information Technology) or computer offices regarding possible cyber attacks by Anonymous or other hacktivists
* Reports, bulletins, warnings and alerts relating to or mentioning Anonymous or hacktivism sent to or received from any federal law enforcement or intelligence agencies or any fusion center
* Incident reports investigating possible hacking activity falling within the office's jurisdiction and allegedly carried out by members of Anonymous or other hacktivist groups
* Materials generated as a result of any suspected or actual compromise, breach or "dox" (revealing of personal or private information) by Anonymous or other hacktivists

Anonymous has been described by government agencies as a non-hierarchical hacktivist collective, Anonymous uses hacking (and arguably cracking) techniques to register political protest in campaigns known as “#ops.” Best known for their distributed denial of services (DDoS) attacks, past activities have included attacks against the Church of Scientology; Visa, Paypal, and others who withdrew their services from WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange after that group began releasing war documents; #OpTunisia and others purporting to support the Arab Spring; and a campaign that brought down the website of the Westboro Baptist Church. #Ops are usually marked with the release of a video of a reader in a Guy Fawkes mask using a computer generated voice. See attached for additional background information.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Lebanon School District/SAU88)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by each school board chair in the district/SAU related to their position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on district or secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

Therefore, this email communication may be subject to public disclosure.
